paragraphs.body: What is your language? This is a common question that is asked around the world. The bigger question must be, what is a language and what makes it different? In the essay, “How to Tame a Wild Tongue”, Gloria Anzaldua shows her view of her language and culture. She shows her language through different sayings and expresses her tongue as a language. She includes that she sometimes does not believe that she has a language because she is in a world that she cannot decide. In a way, a language I believe can be part of who someone is. It can either be because of their culture or something they want to learn down the road. I do in my life have a few experiences with different interactions with my language.

The language I speak with my family, friends, and others around me is called my "home" language. In my life, I have always spoken English as my only language. I use it primarily with all the people in my surroundings because I learned to speak it from an early age. Being currently in college, I often come across the phrase “What’s up?” I notice it when I walk into a building and spot someone I know, or even when I enter my dorm and hear my roommates say it. I never truly grasped why people consistently used that phrase as opposed to asking "How are you?" or another greeting. I often hear it from my male friends, so maybe it is something they picked up in their youth. I did not begin using the phrase until high school when I would walk past others in the hallway. I have become familiar with hearing the word frequently. Eventually, the expression began to resemble home. After I became familiar with the greeting "What's happening?" I no longer wondered how to respond to it. What I discovered about my language is that it can reveal a great deal about a person's upbringing. It displays a variety of cultures that hold great significance for some individuals. Frequently, it can assist individuals in discovering their place in the world, whether they choose to maintain their native language or switch to a different one. It helps them find their self-identity in a way.

Self-identity can be defined as “Self-identity is the identity that one gives oneself. In other words, it is the role or roles which one believes define oneself. This identity influences people to prioritize certain ideas, beliefs, values, and actions” (Hepler). To me, self-identity is an important thing today. My languages have influenced my life by shaping my identity. My journey in acquiring multiple languages has contributed to my development over time, allowing me to broaden my perspectives, cultivate compassion, and enhance my self-awareness. All individuals possess a unique characteristic that defines who they are. Either someone stands out due to their uniqueness or their culture is what truly defines them. The way I perceive things has shaped my ability to maintain a positive attitude towards life. Interacting with others through various expressions and studying the language demonstrates how individuals from diverse backgrounds communicate effectively. I believe that every interaction with someone helps to create a connection. Some interactions with people I have had have been good and bad. Some people can just be and some other people can be very nice and supportive about different things. Establishing a connection can aid in personal growth for both individuals. I believe that understanding the languages we use reveals our individual cultures and diversity. Most of the time, I am a people pleaser when it comes to some things. I would be willing to give up something just so it would make somebody else happy in a way. Our individuality sets us apart from one another and contributes to our diversity. My language has influenced my identity over my life, as I previously stated. In my childhood, I was not exposed to various languages as some other families are. While I did not study a different language, it still united my family in a distinct way.

annotations.text: Some interactions with people I have had have been good and bad. Some people can just be and some other people can be very nice and supportive about different things. Establishing a connection can aid in personal growth for both individuals. I believe that understanding the languages we use reveals our individual cultures and diversity. Most of the time, I am a people pleaser when it comes to some things. I would be willing to give up something just so it would make somebody else happy in a way.

paragraphs.body: To speak English means to Be nice when approached, Do not stare at others, Watch what you say, Mind your business, Someone might be overhearing. Throughout my life I have heard different phrases that also tie into my language and understanding of it. Growing up, I frequently heard the phrase "Don't rock the boat." When I was a child, my mother would say this to me. As a child, I found it challenging at times to navigate the various sports I engaged in and handle the team drama that often occurred during seasons. It was common for teams to have children who constantly strived to be the top performers. When they show a lot of effort, they often end up lowering others' self-esteem and making them feel weak. When others try to be the best at something it can emotionally hurt others in a way by feeling like they are not good enough and it might lower their confidence to do a certain sport. My mother always told me this to prevent further complications with my teammates and other friends. During my time in wrestling, one of my teammates consistently strived to outperform me whenever possible. He would yell, schedule private sessions, and try to be better than the whole class. His actions caused me to feel that the sport no longer fit me. Whenever I began to consider speaking up or causing a disturbance, I would always recall the phrase. I believe my mother's intention in saying this was to advise me against expressing my feelings towards others or causing conflict, as it could lead to further problems. This lesson taught me the importance of focusing on my own problems to avoid unnecessary drama in my life. Being loyal to others and avoiding unnecessary conflicts has benefited my relationships. If I stay out of the drama it helps my relationships by not getting involved and not having to worry about others. This statement highlights the significance for individuals to remain composed in the presence of others, to avoid saying anything that may cause discomfort or distress.

annotations.comment: I'm quite taken with the ideas you're creating here! I'd love to see you use more concrete and specific details taken from your own life and experience (i.e. the details that _only_ you could write) :)

paragraphs.body: For these reasons, this shows how society and self-esteem can show how language and cultures can impact a person. Reflecting on life and showing the different things that happen in it can really show how it affects you and others. Language and culture are big things in society now.
Works Cited Hepler, Reed. “Self-Identity Definition, Theory & Examples.” Study.com, 2022, study.com/learn/lesson/self-identity-theory-examples.html.
